# AWS EFA Receiver

The AWS EFA receiver collects metrics from Amazon Elastic Fabric Adapter (EFA) devices
by reading hardware counters from `/sys/class/infiniband/*/ports/*/hw_counters/`.

EFA is a network device that can be attached to Amazon EC2 instances to accelerate
AI/ML, HPC, and other distributed workloads. This receiver exposes EFA driver metrics
including RDMA operations, network traffic, retransmissions, and connection health.

## Configuration

```yaml
receivers:
  awsefareceiver:
    collection_interval: 60s
    # host_path is the root path to the host filesystem when running in a container.
    # Set to "/host" or "/rootfs" when the host filesystem is mounted into the container.
    host_path: ""
```

## Metrics

All metrics are cumulative monotonic sums (int64) representing EFA driver hardware counters.

> **Note:** Not all 22 counters are present on all EFA driver versions or instance types.
> Missing counters are skipped silently — the receiver emits metrics only for counters
> that exist on the host.

| Metric Name | Description | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| `efa_rdma_read_bytes` | Bytes received using RDMA read operations | By |
| `efa_rdma_write_bytes` | Bytes written by other instances using RDMA write operations | By |
| `efa_rdma_write_recv_bytes` | Bytes received by RDMA write operations | By |
| `efa_rx_bytes` | Bytes received | By |
| `efa_rx_dropped` | Packets received and then dropped | 1 |
| `efa_tx_bytes` | Bytes transmitted | By |
| `efa_retrans_bytes` | EFA SRD bytes retransmitted | By |
| `efa_retrans_pkts` | EFA SRD packets retransmitted | 1 |
| `efa_retrans_timeout_events` | Times EFA SRD traffic timed out | 1 |
| `efa_unresponsive_remote_events` | Times an EFA SRD remote connection was unresponsive | 1 |
| `efa_impaired_remote_conn_events` | Times EFA SRD connections entered impaired state | 1 |
| `efa_tx_pkts` | Packets transmitted | 1 |
| `efa_rx_pkts` | Packets received | 1 |
| `efa_send_bytes` | Bytes sent via send operations | By |
| `efa_recv_bytes` | Bytes received via recv operations | By |
| `efa_send_wrs` | Send work requests completed | 1 |
| `efa_recv_wrs` | Receive work requests completed | 1 |
| `efa_rdma_write_wrs` | RDMA write work requests completed | 1 |
| `efa_rdma_read_wrs` | RDMA read work requests completed | 1 |
| `efa_rdma_write_wr_err` | RDMA write work request errors | 1 |
| `efa_rdma_read_wr_err` | RDMA read work request errors | 1 |
| `efa_rdma_read_resp_bytes` | Bytes sent in RDMA read response | By |

## Data Point Attributes

Each metric data point includes the following attributes to identify the EFA device:

| Attribute | Description |
|---|---|
| `aws.efa.device` | The EFA InfiniBand device name (e.g. `efa_0`) |
| `aws.efa.port` | The EFA port number |
| `aws.efa.eni.id` | The Elastic Network Interface ID associated with the EFA device |
